2605.03514.title: Revisiting Graph-Tokenizing Large Language Models: A Systematic Evaluation of Graph Token Understanding
2605.03514.paper_summary: The remarkable success of large language models (LLMs) has motivated researchers to adapt them as universal predictors for various graph tasks. As a widely recognized paradigm, Graph-Tokenizing LLMs (GTokenLLMs) compress complex graph data into graph tokens and treat them as prefix tokens for querying LLMs, leading many to believe that LLMs can understand graphs more effectively and efficiently. In this paper, we challenge this belief: \textit{Do GTokenLLMs fully understand graph tokens in the natural-language embedding space?} Motivated by this question, we formalize a unified framework for GTokenLLMs and propose an evaluation pipeline, \textbf{GTEval}, to assess graph-token understanding via instruction transformations at the format and content levels. We conduct extensive experiments on 6 representative GTokenLLMs with GTEval. The primary findings are as follows: (1) Existing GTokenLLMs do not fully understand graph tokens. They exhibit over-sensitivity or over-insensitivity to instruction changes, and rely heavily on text for reasoning; (2) Although graph tokens preserve task-relevant graph information and receive attention across LLM layers, their utilization varies across models and instruction variants; (3) Additional instruction tuning can improve performance on the original and seen instructions, but it does not fully address the challenge of graph-token understanding, calling for further improvement.
2605.03514.paper_summary_zh: 大型語言模型（LLMs）的顯著成功激勵研究人員將其調整為各種圖形任務的通用預測器。作為一種廣泛認可的範式，圖形標記化LLMs（GTokenLLMs）將複雜的圖形數據壓縮為圖形標記，並將其視為查詢LLMs的前綴標記，這使得許多人相信LLMs能夠更有效且高效地理解圖形。在本文中，我們挑戰這一信念：\textit{GTokenLLMs是否完全理解自然語言嵌入空間中的圖形標記？} 受到這個問題的啟發，我們為GTokenLLMs形式化了一個統一框架，並提出了一個評估管道\textbf{GTEval}，以通過格式和內容層面的指令轉換來評估圖形標記的理解。我們在6個具有代表性的GTokenLLMs上進行了廣泛的實驗，使用GTEval。主要發現如下：（1）現有的GTokenLLMs並未完全理解圖形標記。它們對指令變更表現出過度敏感或過度不敏感，並在推理中過度依賴文本；（2）儘管圖形標記保留了與任務相關的圖形信息並在LLM層中獲得注意，但其利用在模型和指令變體之間存在差異；（3）額外的指令調整可以改善對原始和已見指令的性能，但並未完全解決圖形標記理解的挑戰，呼籲進一步的改進。
2605.03514.author: Zhongjian Zhang et.al.
2605.03514.authors: Zhongjian Zhang, Yue Yu, Mengmei Zhang, Junping Du, Xiao Wang, Chuan Shi
